file_name: V and S - 2024 - Chronological dingo optimizer-based deep maxout network for skin cancer detection and skin lesion se.json

did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper uses a Chronological Dingo Optimizer (CDO)-based Deep Maxout Network (DMN) for skin cancer detection and a Double U-Net for skin lesion segmentation. These are deep learning and computer vision methods applied to the SIIM-ISIC Melanoma Classification dataset to achieve classification and segmentation results.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper presents original experimental work, including the development of a Chronological Dingo Optimizer (CDO)-based Deep Maxout Network (DMN) and Double U-Net for skin cancer detection and segmentation. It details specific methodologies, experimental setups (Python, Intel core-i3, 4 GB RAM), and reports new quantitative results (sensitivity 0.959, F-measure 0.908, accuracy 0.923, specificity 0.837) on the SIIM-ISIC Melanoma Classification dataset, rather than summarizing prior literature.

title: Chronological Dingo Optimizer‑based Deep Maxout Network for skin cancer detection and skin lesion segmentation using Double U‑Net
brief_summary: This study proposes a Chronological Dingo Optimizer (CDO)-based Deep Maxout Network (DMN) for skin cancer detection and skin lesion segmentation. The methodology employs a Double U-Net for effective lesion segmentation, followed by data augmentation techniques such as shifting, blurring, and rotation. The DMN is optimized using the novel CDO, which integrates chronological concepts with the Dingo Optimizer. The model was evaluated on the SIIM-ISIC Melanoma Classification dataset using accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and F-measure. The proposed scheme achieved a sensitivity of 0.959, an F-measure of 0.908, an accuracy of 0.923, and a specificity of 0.837. These results indicate that the CDO-based DMN provides superior performance for automated skin cancer detection compared to existing methods.

lesion_types_studied.details: The study focuses on detecting melanoma using the SIIM-ISIC Melanoma Classification dataset. The task is framed as a binary classification (skin cancer detection vs. healthy/no cancer) based on the accuracy/sensitivity/specificity metrics reported.

all_task_types.details: The paper proposes a Chronological Dingo Optimizer (CDO)-based Deep Maxout Network (DMN) for skin cancer detection. The task is framed as binary classification (detecting skin cancer/melanoma) evaluated using accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and F-measure on the SIIM-ISIC Melanoma Classification dataset.

The methodology employs a Double U-Net for effective skin lesion segmentation to extract affected areas before classification. This is a secondary task in the pipeline to support the primary detection task.

datasets_used.name: SIIM-ISIC Melanoma Classification dataset
datasets_used.description: A public dataset containing dermoscopic images of skin lesions and associated metadata. The data is provided in DICOM configuration, with images in JPEG and TFRecord formats, and metadata in CSV files. It was accessed via Kaggle.

use.training: Used for training the Chronological Dingo Optimizer-based Deep Maxout Network (CDO-based DMN) and Double U-Net segmentation model.
use.validation: Used for validation during the evaluation of performance indicators (accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, F-measure) with varying learning sets (60 to 90).
use.testing: Used for testing the final performance of the proposed scheme.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report any formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. Performance was evaluated using accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and F-measure without statistical significance testing.

experiment_1_learning_set_90.notes: Performance evaluated by maximizing the learning set from 60 to 90. For learning set 90, CDO_based DMN achieved the highest metrics among comparative traditional methods.

experiment_2_k_fold_9.notes: Performance evaluated using K-fold measure with K=9. CDO_based DMN showed superior performance compared to traditional methods.

experiment_3_algorithmic_analysis_popula.notes: Algorithmic analysis performed by elevating population size from 10 to 40. CDO_based DMN outperformed other optimizer-based DMN variants.

experiment_4_learning_set_90_iteration_1.notes: Specific iteration results for learning set 90. Accuracy peaked at iteration 100 with 0.959. Sensitivity peaked at iteration 100 with 0.960. Specificity peaked at iteration 100 with 0.915. F-measure peaked at iteration 80 with 0.875, then dropped to 0.885 at iteration 100.

architecture_used.main_model: Chronological Dingo Optimizer (CDO)-based Deep Maxout Network (DMN)

architecture_details.activation_function: Max-out function (general type of ReLU executing max operation on altered linear illustrations)
architecture_details.optimizer: Chronological Dingo Optimizer (CDO) (integration of chronological concepts with Dingo Optimizer/DOX)
architecture_details.loss_function: Mean Squared Error (MSE) between actual outcome (Ck) and DMN classifier outcome (Zk): ℑ = (1/l) * Σ(Ck - Zk)²
architecture_details.training_schedule: Learning set maximized from 60 to 90; K-fold value expanded; population size elevated from 10 to 40 for algorithmic analysis

training_details.hardware: Intel core-i3 processor, Windows 10 OS, 4 GB RAM
training_details.preprocessing: Contrast enhancement, mean filter for noise elimination

machine_learning_or_deep_learning_models: Chronological Dingo Optimizer-based Deep Maxout Network (CDO-based DMN)

data_augmentation_techniques.details: Pixels of the image are shifted horizontally without varying the size of the image; some pixels are cut off and new pixels included to maintain dimensions.
Pixels of the image are shifted vertically without varying the size of the image; some pixels are cut off and new pixels included to maintain dimensions.
Images are rotated by a certain angle, applied up to 360 degrees depending upon the object in the image.
Brightness is adjusted to detect objects in low-resolution or varying lighting conditions.
Images are randomly blurred; noise is included to help with blurry images.

conclusions: The proposed Chronological Dingo Optimizer (CDO)-based Deep Maxout Network (DMN) combined with Double U-Net for skin lesion segmentation achieved superior performance in skin cancer detection compared to existing methods.
The model achieved a sensitivity of 0.959, an F-measure of 0.908, an accuracy of 0.923, and a specificity of 0.837 on the SIIM-ISIC Melanoma Classification dataset.
The integration of chronological concepts with the Dingo Optimizer (CDO) effectively optimized the parameters of the Deep Maxout Network.
The Double U-Net was effective for skin lesion segmentation, which facilitated the subsequent detection process.
Data augmentation techniques (shifting, blurring, rotation, brightness adjustment) were utilized to enhance the detection process.
important_remarks: The study was conducted on a low-specification hardware setup (Intel core-i3 processor, 4 GB RAM), which may limit the generalizability of computational efficiency claims to modern clinical or high-performance environments.

The paper does not report formal statistical significance testing (e.g., p-values) to validate the superiority of the proposed CDO-based DMN over comparative methods, relying solely on point estimates of accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and F-measure.

The dataset used is the SIIM-ISIC Melanoma Classification dataset; however, the paper does not specify the exact train/validation/test split ratios or the number of samples per class, raising potential concerns about data leakage or reproducibility.

The model's specificity (0.837) is notably lower than its sensitivity (0.959), indicating a higher rate of false positives, which is a critical consideration for clinical deployment where unnecessary biopsies are a concern.

The paper claims to use clinical details (age, gender, etc.) to avoid affecting accuracy, but the methodology and results sections do not explicitly demonstrate how these metadata features were integrated into the Deep Maxout Network architecture.

Comparative baselines (e.g., RCNN-FKM, CNN, SSO-CSAE) are cited from other studies rather than re-implemented and re-trained on the same split, which may introduce unfair comparisons due to differences in preprocessing, augmentation, or evaluation protocols.

The research lacks external validation on independent, multi-center datasets, limiting the assessment of the model's robustness to variations in image acquisition devices, populations, and skin tones.
